mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Azure services. Key trade-offs include reliance on Azure for management and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure services and manage your AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> This command installs the Azure CLI on Debian-based systems.

steps.title: Create AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This command merges the AKS cluster credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster to optimize resource usage.

```bash

az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

```

> This sets up autoscaling for your AKS cluster.
> This enables monitoring for your AKS cluster.
commands: az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will remove the cluster and all resources associated with it.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Resource Group
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the resource group, which will remove all resources within it.

```bash

az group delete --name myResourceGroup --yes --no-wait

```

> This will permanently delete the resource group and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your workloads and configurations. This command upgrades the AKS cluster to a newer version.

```bash

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

```

> This upgrades the AKS cluster to Kubernetes version 1.31.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are running the new version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that the nodes are updated to the new version.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If issues arise after the upgrade, you may need to roll back to the previous version. Ensure you have a backup.

```bash

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version <previous-version>

```

> Replace <previous-version> with the version you want to roll back to.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available quota in your Azure subscription.

**Fix:**
Check your Azure subscription quotas and adjust your resource requests accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node status NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not able to join the cluster or if there are issues with the underlying VM.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs using 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to enable monitoring'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Azure Monitor service is not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have the necessary permissions and that the Azure Monitor service is available in your region.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure your workloads are compatible with the new scheduling features.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

az monitor autoscale create --resource-group myResourceGroup --resource myAKSCluster --resource-type Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters --name myAutoscale --min-count 1 --max-count 5 --count 3
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
